details: The "Bhraman-Darshan Yojana" was launched by the Animal and Fisheries Resources Department, Government of Bihar aims to educate fish farmers in Bihar about the latest fisheries techniques through organized excursions, enabling them to adopt and implement these technologies in their own water sources. The scheme targets 12,000 fish farmers, providing them with a two-day educational tour within the state.
benefits: Increase in fish production and productivity.
Increase in the annual income of fish farmers.
eligibility: The fish farmer should be a resident of Bihar.
The fish farmer should work in private, leased, or government ponds/watersheds.
The fish farmer should be an active member of Block Level Fishermen Cooperation Committees.
The progressive fish farmers who have successfully undergone training under departmental schemes are eligible.
The fish farmer wants to do fish farming and have adequate fish resources available.
The fish farmer can avail the benefit of this scheme only once.

online: and clicks the "Applications are being invited for all the schemes of the financial year (2024-25),
Select the category: Individual and complete all basic details of the applicant for registration, including category, caste, applicant name, date of birth, address, bank details, etc.
Enter a valid 10-digit mobile number and click to send OTP.
An OTP will be sent to the provided mobile number.
Enter the OTP; after verification of the OTP, you will receive a Registration Number and Password.
Click on the "Login" and enter the Registration Number and Password
: Select "Scheme name" under the "Marai Training Scheme" option on the top menu.
Applicant need to provide the following details
Click on the submit button and a receipt will be generated.

faqs.question: Who is eligible to apply for the scheme?
faqs.answer: Fish farmers using private, leased, or government ponds/watersheds and those under various departmental schemes.

faqs.question: How many fish farmers will benefit from the scheme?
faqs.answer: A total of 12,000 fish farmers will benefit from the scheme.

faqs.question: Will there be any registration fee for the excursion program?
faqs.answer: Yes, fish farmers will deposit ₹100 per farmer as a registration fee.

faqs.question: What facilities will be provided during the tour?
faqs.answer: Transportation, food, and accommodation will be provided.

faqs.question: How will the fish farmers be selected for the tour?
faqs.answer: Selection will be based on online applications received through the departmental website.

faqs.question: Who will monitor the implementation of the scheme?
faqs.answer: The Joint Director of Fisheries (Training and Extension) will monitor the scheme.

faqs.question: Is there any provision for incentivizing unit owners/operators?
faqs.answer: Yes, an incentive amount will be given to unit owners/operators who provide detailed technical information during the tour.

faqs.question: Where can applications for the scheme be submitted?
faqs.answer: Applications can be submitted online at fisheries.bihar.gov.in.

faqs.question: Who is responsible for the successful implementation of the scheme?
faqs.answer: The Director of Fisheries is fully accountable for the successful implementation of the scheme.

faqs.question: How will the registration fee be handled if a beneficiary does not participate?
faqs.answer: The registration fee will be confiscated if the beneficiary does not participate.
